item.title: Demolition, Blackouts & Shirley Crash
item.ns0:title: Demolition, Blackouts & Shirley Crash
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, a government demolition exercise in an unregulated community turns physical as over 200 residents scramble to salvage their belongings from the rubble. We investigate a weekend of widespread power outages across the capital that impacted critical healthcare services and left thousands in the dark during a sweltering heatwave. Our team also brings you a somber update on the Shirley Street tragedy as the death toll rises and a 19-year-old faces multiple charges in court. Plus, we highlight a historic scientific discovery by a young Bahamian researcher and join regional leaders in St. Lucia for the opening of the 51st CARICOM Heads of Government meeting.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News, a government demolition exercise in an unregulated community turns physical as over 200 residents scramble to salvage their belongings from the rubble. We investigate a weekend of widespread power outages across the capital that impacted critical healthcare services and left thousands in the dark during a sweltering heatwave. Our team also brings you a somber update on the Shirley Street tragedy as the death toll rises and a 19-year-old faces multiple charges in court. Plus, we highlight a historic scientific discovery by a young Bahamian researcher and join regional leaders in St. Lucia for the opening of the 51st CARICOM Heads of Government meeting.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News, a government demolition exercise in an unregulated community turns physical as over 200 residents scramble to salvage their belongings from the rubble. We investigate a weekend of widespread power outages across the capital that impacted critical healthcare services and left thousands in the dark during a sweltering heatwave. Our team also brings you a somber update on the Shirley Street tragedy as the death toll rises and a 19-year-old faces multiple charges in court. Plus, we highlight a historic scientific discovery by a young Bahamian researcher and join regional leaders in St. Lucia for the opening of the 51st CARICOM Heads of Government meeting.

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
